Title: Mass Transport of Proform of a KDEL-tailed Cysteine Proteinase (SH-EP) to Protein Storage Vacuoles by Endoplasmic Reticulum–derived Vesicle Is Involved in Protein Mobilization in Germinating Seeds

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; ER-derived KDEL-tailed cysteine proteinase-accumulating vesicle (KV); protein storage vacuole (as mature enzyme)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in cotyledons of germinating seeds, detected strongly in day-3 cotyledons
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cotyledon cells of germinating seeds, especially cells undergoing storage protein mobilization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: storage globulin / seed storage proteins (e.g., 7S globulin)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen and amino acid reserve for seedling growth
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Protein storage vacuoles in cotyledon cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rapid mobilization and degradation of storage proteins in germinating seed cotyledons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SH-EP is the major vacuolar cysteine proteinase responsible for degradation of storage proteins in germinated Vigna mungo cotyledons. 2. Its proform accumulates in ER-derived vesicles (KV), bypasses the Golgi, and is delivered massively to protein storage vacuoles. 3. After delivery to PSV, SH-EP is activated and contributes to rapid degradation of storage globulin during germination.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunogold electron microscopy with anti-proform and anti-mature SH-EP antibodies; immunoblotting; ultrastructural analysis; double labeling with storage protein marker
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination after imbibition; cotyledons sampled 1-3 days post-imbibition in darkness at 27°C
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate seed reserve mobilization vigor and germination-associated nutrient remobilization, but constitutive misexpression could cause premature storage protein degradation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SH-EP is a protease, within this paper it is the central effector of storage protein mobilization and is studied mainly as a transport/zymogen cargo with KDEL-dependent sequestration in ER-derived KV rather than as a classical biosynthetic pathway enzyme.
